items.title: FINAL FANTASY XIV: Dawntrail – The Art of Succession -Relics of Eternity- Book Launches Dec. 15th, 2026
items.content:
0 lightbox#closeBackground keyup@window->lightbox#closeWithKeyboard" class="hidden animated fadeIn fixed inset-0 overflow-y-auto items-center justify-center z-9999"> SQUARE ENIX® recently announced that the English-language publication for volume two of the official art book set for FINAL FANTASY XIV: Dawntrail is set to release on December 15, 2026.

Following the release of The Art of Succession -Relics of Heritage-, the upcoming volume, -Relics of Eternity-, features hundreds of pages of full-color art and an exclusive in-game item code for a Lolly Cat minion.

FINAL FANTASY XIV: Dawntrail – The Art of Succession -Relics of Eternity- (English Edition)

Follow the Warrior of Light and their comrades on a journey through the lands of Tural and beyond. This collector’s quality art book offers a wealth of illustrations depicting characters, environments, equipment, and more. The Art of Succession -Relics of Eternity- primarily covers content from Patch 7.1 through Patch 7.5, including The Arcadion, Echoes of Vana’diel, and Cosmic Exploration.

Direct an issue or corrections to the author. Abuse/misuse of this feature carries penalties.

items.title: Final Fantasy XIV: Dawntrail - The Art of Succession -Relics of Eternity- Announced
items.summary: Square Enix has announced Final Fantasy XIV: Dawntrail - The Art of Succession -Relics of Eternity- will release on December 15th, 2026.
items.content:
Square Enix has announced Final Fantasy XIV: Dawntrail - The Art of Succession -Relics of Eternity- will release on December 15th, 2026 .

The book features hundreds of pages of full-cover art, and includes an exclusive item code for a Lolly Cat minion for use within Final Fantasy XIV.

It will retail for $39.99 , and can be purchased via the Official Store Page .

items.title: Wisdom of Nym: Speculating on Final Fantasy XIV Evercold’s weekly activity system
items.content:
So one of the very specific things I said when I was talking about Final Fantasy XIV’s last fan festival was cautioning people about getting out over their skis regarding the weekly activity system that’s coming in Evercold . We currently know very, very little about it from the base level, and thus it is a bad idea to speak too confidently about any of it because… we know very little about it. Presumably we’ll learn more in July. But here I am, writing a column in which I am knowingly getting out over my skis a bit.

In part, this is a deliberate maneuver because I am writing this as much as anything to demonstrate the known unknowns. But it’s also because I feel like doing a bit of speculating and seeing how close to the intent I actually manage to hit. So let’s talk about it, and the first thing I’m going to do is say that the most obvious source we have for the activity system is not something we should actually use as a source.

One of the slides from Yoshida’s keynote specifically shows off a page full of weekly activities as a mockup, and this is the most obvious source for speculating about what the weekly system will look like. However, I am honestly pretty certain that it was never meant to be anything more than a mockup to show what the interface will look like, rather than any sort of actual sample. For example, one of its items is “Embark on an Ocean Fishing voyage,” and while that one is cut off at the bottom of the shot, we can see that it’s offering as many points as an instanced dungeon.

So if you’re using that image as your basis on values or expectations, you should not be. It is not accurate and honestly does not pretend that it is showing you anything beyond a mockup. It’s why it was shown once and then not again. It is a demonstration of what the interface will look like, not what it will be or what it will reward, and thus nothing that is on there should be taken in absolute terms.

Where, then, do we start? Well, I would say that the smartest place to start is actually with our current system because that should give us some kind of baseline.

As it currently stands, players can earn 450 time-limited tomestones per week. If we assume that the players doing so are also earning weekly rewards as they are relevant elsewhere (and we pretend that you can wear two rings from the tomestone vendor, which you can’t, but bear with me), it takes a little over 12 weeks to get a full set of gear including your weapons from zero. So let’s make 12 weeks a baseline. This works pretty nicely, as it happens; there are 12 slots for you to equip something, after all, even if only one combat job reliably puts anything in the offhand slot, so it feels broadly “right” in this sense.

This broadly corresponds to the larger interface mockup, which features its milestones at increments of 3000 from what we can see. And I would go so far as to venture that we can expect, on a weekly basis, for the same basic rules to apply as they currently do, so five Expert dungeons in a week will get you to that milestone.

Keep in mind that at this point, we do not know what “expert” dungeons will look like if, indeed, they even still exist or use that name. Again, this is strictly about the basic shapes, and we haven’t been told that content itself is wildly changing so much as the ways in which we get rewards from it will change.

All of this is based on the assumption that our expected weekly activities will look the same as they do now if that’s how we choose to play. But it’s also assuming that our expected weekly activities are also confined to top-level play, and it’s here that I think there’s the possibility for something more interesting to go on. I just mentioned that we’re talking about “five Expert dungeons will cap you on rewards,” but there are two possible implementations here. The first – if point values stay the same – would be that “clear an Expert dungeon” is worth 600 points and can be repeated. But the second is that “clear an Expert dungeon,” “clear an instanced dungeon,” and “clear a max-level dungeon” are all individual objectives – or all of the aforementioned along with “clear a roulette” and “clear content with random party members.”

The thing is, doing an Expert dungeon would count for all of those activities. But it’s very possible for other things to count for several as well. If you’re doing leveling dungeons on other jobs, you’re still clearing instanced dungeons! And I suspect that’s part of the point.

While I can’t be certain at this time, my suspicion is that stacking different objectives is part of the end goal with this new system, but also in a way that doesn’t force you to choose quite as often. As it stands now, committing to level up a new job means that you have to do a lot of content on top of what you also will be doing in terms of endgame maintenance. Not an onerous burden by any means, but it does mean that if you have less time you have to choose between leveling or getting tomes.

I’m hopeful, albeit not certain, that one of the goals with weekly activity as a system is to change that. That’s not to say that it’ll be faster to level up another job than to just do Expert roulette, as I posited before; rather, it’s that if you’re already doing another job, you can get something out of it in terms of gear progress as well. You don’t have to do as many dungeons in a week to cap out your gear progression because it’s your overall activity that matters most.

That is an exciting prospect to me. As someone who generally engages with the game very broadly, I am fond of feeling like all of it feeds together more than it currently does. In some ways, it’s a mirror of something I’ve already expressed joy over when it comes to the game’s flexibility in its endgame. It is something that could use a refresh, and I’m hopeful to see something that promotes a healthy engagement with the game as a whole instead of a strict regimen – something that I think is broadly within the realm of possibility, obviously.

Or maybe it will just be “you can do your routine of experts all at once instead” and nothing will really change. I’ve been wrong before!

items.title: Another fat cat for the collection is included with FINAL FANTASY XIV: DAWNTRAIL – THE ART OF SUCCESSION -RELICS OF ETERNITY
items.content:
Today, Square Enix announced that the English version of FINAL FANTASY XIV: DAWNTRAIL – THE ART OF SUCCESSION -RELICS OF ETERNITY will release on December 15th, 2026. The art book includes an in-game item code for a Lolly Cat minion.

The book is priced at $39.99 and is available for pre-order from the Square Enix Store, Penguin Random House, and other major booksellers. For more on all things Final Fantasy XIV, stay tuned to GamingTrend.

items.title: Square Enix Manga & Books Announces Final Fantasy XIV: Dawntrail – The Art of Succession -Relics of Eternity- Artbook
items.summary: 2nd artbook in 2-volume set ships in December // Square Enix Manga & Books announced on Thursday it will release the Final Fantasy XIV: Dawntrail – The...
items.content:
Square Enix Manga & Books announced on Thursday it will release the Final Fantasy XIV: Dawntrail – The Art of Succession -Relics of Eternity- artbook on December 15.

The artbook is the second volume of the two-volume set of official artbooks for the Final Fantasy XIV: Dawntrail expansion. The book features 304 pages of full-color artwork featuring characters, environments, equipment, and more.

The book also includes a code for the in-game Lolly Cat minion item.

Square Enix published the first volume of the two-volume set, The Art of Succession –Relics of Heritage– , in April 2025.

Square Enix announced in April the next expansion for Square Enix 's Final Fantasy XIV MMORPG, which is titled "Evercold," will launch in January 2027.

Square Enix released Final Fantasy XIV Online for personal computers in September 2010. After criticism from fans, the company revealed an updated version of the game titled Final Fantasy XIV: A Realm Reborn that added a new world and story. Final Fantasy XIV: A Realm Reborn launched on Windows PC and PlayStation 3 in August 2013, on PlayStation 4 in April 2014, on PlayStation 5 in May 2020, and on Xbox Series X|S in March 2024. Square Enix has since released five expansions: "Heavensward" in June 2015, "Stormblood" in June 2017, "Shadowbringers" in July 2019, "Endwalker" in December 2021, and "Dawntrail" in July 2024. The game has recorded just under 35 million registered accounts worldwide as of April 24.

The game is getting a mobile version for iOS and Android devices. Lightspeed Studios is developing the game, with the game officially licensed from and supervised by Square Enix . The game will have "multiple playtests" in China, and will also launch in China first, with a worldwide release planned after the China launch.

Square Enix announced in February 2022 that it will continue support for the MMORPG for the next 10 years.

Sources: Square Enix Manga & Books ' X/Twitter account , E-mail correspondence

items.title: Final Fantasy XIV Collaborates With Shibuya To Give Goodies In Return For Tax Contributions
items.summary: Final Fantasy XIV has worked together with Shibuya, the famous Tokyo neighborhood, to provide
items.content:
Victoria Rose Posted: Jun 18, 2026 2:45 PM ET Category: News 0

Final Fantasy XIV players living in Japan can get some free goodies just by doing their civic duties. Spotted and shared by Famitsu, FFXIV has worked together with Shibuya, the famous Tokyo neighborhood, to provide “hometown tax” incentives to those who qualify and apply.

As introduced in Japan in 2008, the “hometown tax” allows residents to divert the upcoming year’s tax payments over a certain threshold into up to five specific regions. In exchange, the town sends goods considered culturally or economically relevant to that locale. It’s intended as a way for those who’ve traveled into those populated regions to directly economically support those regions, in exchange for far cheaper acquisition of those goods.

It seems Shibuya has found a way to loop the massive game studio into their own incentives. Square Enix itself contributes not only in its presence as a studio, but also by running events throughout Tokyo and even a store in the Shibuya neighborhood. This isn't the city's first time offering such, as apparently, one gacha game creator offered in-game currency for this tax.

There are several pre-existing items up for grabs through the system, specifically a Fantasia five-pack, the Mameshiba mount, either version of the Collegiate attire, a four-pack of dance emotes, and the Magitek Attire, all for varying tax donation amounts.

If you live in Japan and want to contribute, the Shibuya City Office has set up an official page about the collaboration that even goes as far as explaining how to request Shibuya as a hometown tax recipient.

In the fantasy world of FFXIV , we’re awaiting the Beastmaster limited job and the Evercold expansion not long after, including a major combat overhaul.

Victoria has professionally written about games since 2016, including small former tenures with Polygon and Fanbyte. She mostly spends time in FFXIV, head-deep in roleplay campaigns or stubbornly playing Black Mage through high-end raids. Former obsessions include Dota 2 and The Secret World (also mostly roleplaying). Come visit their estate: Diabolos (Crystal DC), Goblet, Ward 4, Plot 28.

items.title: This Final Fantasy game is offering players rewards for doing their taxes
items.summary: The free goodies include in-game cosmetics and a giant dog companion that can be used as a mount.
items.content:
By Elijah Gonzalez Published Jun 17, 2026, 11:16 AM EDT News They'll also receive in-game costumes and Phials of Fantasia

Capcom pulls Dragon's Dogma 2's controversial microtransactions ahead of DLC launch All active Heartopia codes for free items Crimson Desert patch turns the game into a total pet paradise Japanese Final Fantasy 14 players can earn an adorable giant dog by paying their taxes Image: Square Enix Final Fantasy 14 Sign in to your Polygon.com account Final Fantasy 14 devotees rejoice: you can now earn $88 worth of in-game cosmetics by paying your taxes. Well, if you live in Japan, anyway. Courtesy of Famitsu , Square Enix has teamed up with Tokyo’s Shibuya Ward to offer digital items to Japanese citizens who contribute part of their taxes to the district.

Japan has what’s called the hometown tax contribution system , which allows participants to allocate part of their income taxes to a specific town of their choice, even if they don't currently live there. The initiative was primarily created to help support rural communities suffering from depopulation. When a town or city receives these tax allocations, they tend to send the contributor gifts, such as local specialty goods, or in this case, nerd stuff.

In recent years, some communities have taken to offering digital awards, like how Shibuya Ward previously partnered with the mobile games Monster Strike and Kotodaman . For those who haven’t heard of Shibuya, it’s a famous part of Tokyo traditionally known for its overlap with otaku culture, including anime and video games.

As for the specific Final Fantasy 14 items offered as a reward for Shibuya tax contributions, players will receive five Phials of Fantasia (these are used to change your characters’ appearance), the Collegiate Attire slacks and skirt, the Magitek costume, four dance emotes, and best of all, the Megashiba mount. This large dog is a reference to the Shibuya mascot Hachiko, an Akita Inu that earned a bronze statue for its Argos-like loyalty to its owner. While the items offered aren't exclusive to those who contribute to Shibuya, the ward is likely to see at least a few dedicated FF14 heads sending their taxes for these e-goods.

Earlier this month, Final Fantasy 14 received its 7.51 patch, which added new Ultimate raid challenges featuring Final Fantasy 6 antagonist Kefka Palazzo; it's the second of four updates that Square has promised for this year. The game’s previous major expansion, Dawntrail , launched in 2024, and its upcoming add-on Evercold is set for 2027. Additionally, the game is coming to the Nintendo Switch 2 this August.

items.title: Sarah’s Final Fantasy XIV Fan Fest Experience
items.summary: What is the most magical event full of sparkle and wonder that only takes place every couple of years? Why, it's FFXIV FanFest! Sarah Ferries provides a journal of her experience at the event, including the Jollibee and Requiem Café visits.
items.content:
Let’s just start off by saying that the weather was way better in Los Angeles than the previous Fan Fests in Las Vegas. Yes, things like hotels were more expensive, but I would take that any day over the 100+-degree heat. Even Yoshi P (Producer and Director Naoki Yoshida) said he was melting!

I arrived about 8:30am for the 10am keynote to get a good seat. Just walking into the stage area, it felt a lot bigger than in previous years in Vegas. I was in my Pictomancer cosplay, which was a bit chilly, but better than being too hot. It did get warmer as the keynote went on, though. It truly was jam-packed and longer than a normal keynote. I was intending to post updates on social media like normal, but the internet was garbage inside the convention center so I just made notes on my phone, and was at least able to use Discord. That said, I also forgot to get the media-only WiFi information, so that’s partly on me.

Most of the keynote people loved. There was lots of cheering and clapping, especially with the Evercold trailer . However, when it came to showcasing the Reborn/Evolved modes, the people around me didn’t appear to like it, especially after Yoshi P announced that all new jobs will be only using the Evolved mode. I think it was the initial shock of changing things that really set people off, but after talking through it with people, it doesn’t seem like it’s going to be as big of a change as some people think. I was able to meet up with a lot of my friends from Twitch chats and Final Fantasy XIV, and after we all talked about it, I think it will be better. As someone who plays on a controller, this might be of particular benefit to me. I have my own rotation, and I have no idea if that’s the “correct” Pictomancer rotation, but with the evolved mode it might allow for more flexibility.

After the welcome ceremony, I went with a group of friends to Jollibee, which was holding a Final Fantasy XIV collaboration for Fan Fest. Even though the Roulette Game Show looked fun, we were all very hungry. We arrived at Jollibee and it fortunately wasn’t that busy. I think we only waited about a half hour. We wanted to go to this exact one, because we were told this was the only one that was fully decorated. I was amused at G’raha Tia being on the trash cans eating a sandwhich. This seemed to be the best time to go, since outside of Fan Fest people were saying that the lines at other times were many hours long. I got the $21 box to get my fancy emote. I only ate the sandwich there, but it was delicious. I really liked the sauce, and it came with the good reminder that no chocobos were harmed in the making of the food. We were wondering what they were going to do with the decorations after the event was over. I would’ve loved to take one of the aetheryte lights and put it in my room.

We made it back to Fan Fest around 3pm, and I wanted to see some of the developer panel to understand the Evolved mode a little better, but then I got distracted by shiny cosplayers and friends. Unfortunately, by around 4pm my feet hurt so bad I could not walk, which is partly the price I paid for wearing my cosplay shoes, and I ended up going back to my hotel to put on my sneakers before coming back. I was able to get back just in time to watch the “Glamoured to Life” cosplay walk for around twenty minutes before having to go to my meet and greet. I am glad I was able to see some of the streamers I watch walk, including my favorite scene I was able to see: Sultana drinking from the goblet on stage. The crowd reaction was great. She came out and pulled out the cup, at which point you could hear the gasps and the air leave the room. Then she went to drink, to which everyone immediately screamed, and then stood there for a second before giving a thumbs up. That got a lot of cheers.

When I got to my meet and greet, I am surprised how fast and easy it was. At bigger cons, these things are a mess and you often have to wait hours to meet your chosen guest. However, at Fan Fest you simply had to line up fifteen minutes before your appointment, and then go through relatively fast. I was able to meet Jason Charles Miller, who originally voiced Raubahn Aldynn in A Realm Reborn, but is perhaps better known for performing the vocals in the theme songs for Shadowbringers and Endwalker, plus “Unleashed” from Dawntrail . He was very nice and I even got to chat with him a little bit while my photo was taken.

The Piano Performance with Keiko and special guest Amanda Achen was out of this world. As always, Keiko has a beautiful princess dress for the performance. The sound and stage quality was fantastic. There were some camera issues, where the shot stayed on Keiko for a very long time even when Amanda was singing. I’m chalking that up to technical issues, hopefully. I’m so glad I was able to see the stage from my seat so I could see Amanda singing. Luckily, it wasn’t most of the concert, so I was able to watch the big screens on the sides of the stage.

I was able to attend a portion of “From Tacos to Tenders: Serving up the Lyrics of FFXIV ” with Michael-Christopher Koji Fox. The man is hilarious, and I love listening to him talk about the game, the antics, and how he comes up with lyrics. This panel showed how Square Enix really listens to its players as well as showcasing famous misheard lyrics to some of the songs from the game. One of the most misheard lyrics is from Ultima theme, with players chanting along with “big fat tacos” instead of “Beat, the Heart of Sabik.” Typing it out, it seems like a bizarre mishearing, but you’d have to hear it. He then had fans come up on stage to write new lyrics to “eScape”, which the audience voted on. The winning set of lyrics was actually changed for the Primals live concert at the end of the night! VTuber IronMouse was a special guest during this panel and gave a small concert at the end which was actually really good. I had never seen her before, but I liked her performance.

As much as I wanted to see random Q&As with Yoshi P, I hadn’t yet got the chance to play any of the games yet. I heard a lot of them were capping lines at 4:30, so wanted to get as much done as possible, though I am sad I missed the question about the butt slider.

I started with Run Alpaca Run, which is pretty much a carnival race horse game where you roll a ball into various holes: think of a mini version of Skee-Ball. I did not win, but it was still fun. I wasn’t able to get into the Crystalline Conflict Palaistra Challenge, as the line was already too long, and it’s not something I got into in-game, so it’s hard to compare the physical game to the digital area. I did try the Pelupelu Pitch, which is another carnival style game of ring toss. I am also terrible at this game and scored zero points, but it was still fun. Next was Mica’s Magical Maneuvers, which was a reaction-time based game. Sticks drop suddenly and you have to grab them before they hit the floor. Metem’s Limit Break Challenge was another carnival-style game I did not partake in, because of my tiny, sensitive hands. It gives participants one opening and a single punch to score big DPS; I watched a guy punch a score of 649, which was pretty high compared to the others. However, I did try the Arcadion Slam to try out for the Solution Nine Yans; shooting hoops with a basketball that couldn’t go wrong, right? Wrong! In my haste of trying to make baskets in competition with other people on a time limit, the ball bounced off the rim and hit me in the face. Luckily, it didn’t leave a bruise or a bloody nose, just hurt pride and some redness. Last but not least, Ride the Rhythm was the most popular game at Fan Fest. The line was always long, but it was definitely the highlight. This was basically a disco floor that people had to dance on, but with elements from a boss fight, complete with area of effect squares, tankbuster circles, and a stack marker. It was really cool to see boss mechanics from in the game come to life in person. There was one thing I didn’t get to do but I wish I had: the photo spot with G’raha Tia on a gondola. That was probably the second most popular thing at Fan Fest. I did get a photo at Aunt Tii’s Tacos in Tuliyollal, though.

Having Fan Fest modeled after the latest expansion, Dawntrail , and showcasing its main areas like Solution Nine and Tuliyollal was done really well. There were lots of photo opportunities with the giant “XIV” letters, well known landmarks like Aunt Tii’s Tacos, different Solution Nine areas such as a lounge and bar, and even a photo op with Honey B. Lovely, everyone’s favorite boss. (Alright, not everyone’s, but she was pretty popular.) The only thing I would’ve liked more of was an expanded Solution Nine lounge with more couches and chairs, as they were always full of people, meaning many had to sit along the walls or on the floor.

The last hurrah of the evening was a concert from The Primals, the official band of Final Fantasy XIV lead by Sound Director Masayoshi Soken since 2014. The members are Masayoshi Soken, Michael-Christopher Koji Fox, GUNN, Eikichi Iwai, and Tetsuya Tachibana. Last Fan Fest, people brought their own light sticks for the Primals concert and this year Square Enix decided to sell them at their merch station, which made it that much more fun and I was able to get one of the last light sticks before they sold out. There were multiple colors to choose from on the light stick to match the songs the Primals were playing, and everyone in the crowd seemed to be on the same page. The concert began with the “checking member status” display on the video screens, as one would see waiting for a dungeon to fill in game. The band began “Open Sky”, followed by “Scream”, “Ride the Rhythym”, “Not Afraid” (with special guest Yoshi P), “Unleashed”, “Shadowbringers”, “Absolute Tyranny”, “A Long Fall”, “Rise”, “eScape”, and finally “Under the Weight”. I was surprised when the concert ended as I was enjoying it so much I wasn’t ready for it to end.

Before I left Anaheim, I was able to visit the FFXIV Requiem Café. There was not a lot of merch left, and all the pastries and some of the drinks were sold out. I was able to grab a New Adventures drink, which had a sprout bookmark in the whipped cream atop a refreshing mint citrus soda, and the Scree! ice cream drink. This was a brown sugar milk tea with chocolate drizzle topped with a toasted marshmallow cold foam, gold lustre dust, and a serpent gummy. Both of these were VERY delicious. The inside of the café was magical and wondrous, with a fake giant tree that looked like a weeping willow with a few seats inside. It had a TV showing some story quests, as well as many cardboard stand ups of our favorite scions. The store was decked out with gaming computers that had the free trial loaded up for people to play the game, along with a post-it wall for gamers to leave their mark. I wish I could take the tree with me and put it in my house!

Overall this year’s Fan Fest was yards above the Las Vegas one in 2023. The weather was much nicer, there was a bigger space that was laid out much better, and there were better food options. As always, thank you Square Enix for putting on these events!

items.title: Choose My Adventure: Cutting through the latest normal raids of Final Fantasy XIV Dawntrail
items.content:
She braced through the buzzing strain of the gunblade’s explosive strike, the slashing of steel and roar of fire just covering the monster’s bellow of pain. The beast retaliated almost immediately, swinging with the blind rage borne of agony at her. She barely fired off a magical shell from the weapon that brought up a half dome of aether, taking the brunt of the clubbing swing, though some still managed to push through.

A soft, familiar glow of energy swiftly enveloped her, injecting new vigor into weary sinew and encouraging her to push on. Her strikes paired with the magical burst from the red-haired Miqo’te just behind and the wild and dusty-looking Hyur who looked for all the world like a whirlwind of matched swords.

The thrill of battle swelled back into the Roegadyn’s heart. She remembered why she even thought to take up this path. At least for a brief moment. There was a fight to win, after all.

Before I recount my time in Final Fantasy XIV’s most recent raids, I wanted to offer some thanks to those who provided words of encouragement. Things locally are still not better, but they were improved enough that I was ready to get back in to this week’s tasks, and those who did leave some messages did a lot to buoy me.

What also helped me push in to these encounters was having some good friends tag along, namely my husband and a mutual pair of friends. I always seem to find more courage when I do new-to-me things with familiar people around. So it was that we started first with the Arcadion.

For context to regular players, I got as far as M7 when the series was more current, but the Brute Abombinator proved to be something of a wall for me and mine at the time. Of course, increased item levels and more knowledge of the fight meant that this return trip was much smoother. Still, I admit to some trepidation about going back in, but I’m glad to report that getting my face caved in as frequently as I had previously meant I also had some muscle memory to fall back on.

From there, it continued to move fairly smoothly as the rest of the sci-fantasy pro wrestlers juiced up with monstrous souls came one by one. Each fight was better than the last, while Tyrant proved to be a stern challenge. And then at the Lindwurm things kind of… I don’t want to say petered out, but it was certainly less taxing by comparison. Narratively speaking, it was a bit of a victory lap, but it still had fun mechanics.

Of course, where this series of fights really shined was in the vibe. I used to be a big pro wrestling fan and still dabble now and again to this day, so watching the whole thing play out was really a treat. And I cannot possibly write about this raid series without noting the music. Every song was awesome, but personal props definitely go to Sinister and The Mirror .

Once I got through those fights without too much damage, I felt my confidence grow fairly significantly. Even when I was trying to beat myself down, the people who joined me made sure to talk me away from self-flagellation, kept me in check, and reminded me of context. This was all-new to me. I was doing fine. Nobody else is mad at me.

It’s really easy to just ignore that advice, but it was really heartening to be reminded in the moment. Some days it’s easier than others to forget that most people in FFXIV aren’t judgmental little goblins. Someone in the PUG chat even welcomed me in to a new fight at one point.

The following day, we went in to the Vana’diel raids. As I mentioned before, I had at least unlocked The Second Walk, and going in I once more felt a little bit of creeping worry, particularly since I was the main tank for my third of the 24-player team. At least during the bosses I was able to just be a DPS with sterner defenses.

These raids were, by and large, a bit of a stiffer test for me, particularly the final fight in The Second Walk and the penultimate fight in The Third Walk. In the case of the former, I at least could kind of understand what it was that I was doing wrong, but the latter kind of baffled me. I had to be scraped off of the floor multiple times. Six, to be exact. My penchant for self-deprecation made sure to keep track.

Once again, I’m glad I had the support I did, both direct and indirect. For example, pretty much my entire portion of the raid team and several others in the whole squad were being slapped around, and one of the helpers was definitely quiet and tense because, like, this dude was freaking hard. I’m at least willing to practice more and try again. Especially if it means I get that emote.

While the vibes of the Arcadion series were immaculate, the whole feeling through Echoes of Vana’diel was one of general indifference to me. This is not to suggest that I’m going to discount Final Fantasy XI out of hand, especially since it was a formative game that really got me in to MMORPGs. More than anything, though, the storyline that wove through the whole thing just felt a bit throwaway.

items.title: FINAL FANTASY RESONANCE is available for pre-purchase now!
items.summary: https://store.steampowered.com/app/3259780/ What if FINAL FANTASY had continued to push the limits of pixel art and evolved from its classic roots? The first FINAL FANTASY to join the HD‑2D lineup, FINAL FANTASY RESONANCE delivers a visuall
items.content:
https://store.steampowered.com/app/3259780/ What if FINAL FANTASY had continued to push the limits of pixel art and evolved from its classic roots? The first FINAL FANTASY to join the HD‑2D lineup, FINAL FANTASY RESONANCE delivers a visually striking experience that feels both nostalgic and new, enhanced by dynamic camera angles and pixelized 3D models that make every moment more dramatic and immersive. Return to the pixel‑art world of FINAL FANTASY, evolved from its classic roots. Travel with companions bound by fate and call on Visions to aid you in battle to uncover the truth behind a force that threatens to bring everything to ruin.

Digital Deluxe Edition:

This product is a bundle that includes the FINAL FANTASY RESONANCE base game along with the Magitek & Grimoire Deluxe Pack, which contains an item that transforms your chocobo's appearance into magitek armor, an item exclusive to this pack that unlocks a certain character's Fel Meteor Limit Burst, and useful items to aid you at the start of your journey. Digital Deluxe Edition Contents: ▼ FINAL FANTASY RESONANCE Base Game ▼ Magitek & Grimoire Deluxe Pack *Images are for illustrative purposes only. Actual in‑game visuals may differ. 　 Pack Contents: 　 • Magitek Armor Key x1 　 • Archwitch’s Grimoire x1 　 • Mastery Ring x1 (Increases MRP gained in battle by a certain amount) 　 • Tent x5 　 • Mist Potion x5 　 • Mist Ether x5 　 • Phoenix Down x5 　 • Remedy x3

Pre-order Bonus: Magitek Airship & Squire Starter Pack

*Images are for illustrative purposes only. Actual in‑game visuals may differ. This set contains an item that transforms your airship’s appearance into a magitek vessel, along with useful items to aid you at the start of your journey. Pack Contents: • Magitek Airship Passkey x1 • Knight’s Greatsword x1 • Chestplate of Preparation x1 (Increases EXP gained in battle by a certain amount) • Magicite Shard x1 • Tent x3 • Mist Potion x3

items.title: Square Enix Releases Final Fantasy XIV Online Switch 2 Gameplay Trailer
items.content:
During the Nintendo Direct this week, Square Enix's upcoming release Final Fantasy XIV Online featured in a sizzle reel towards the end of the presentation.

Although it was only on display for a moment, it was arguably the best look at this version of the game yet. Now, Square Enix has released a gameplay trailer of the online multiplayer title running on the Switch 2. It's got some quick segments of gameplay, mixed with in-game cutscenes.

In case you missed it, this title will be getting an "early access" release on the Switch 2 in August 2026 . Here are some additional details from Square Enix's support page about the early access period, or if you're a player coming across from another platform:

Those playing FINAL FANTASY XIV for the first time on Nintendo Switch 2 Purchase FINAL FANTASY XIV for Nintendo Switch 2 and a subscription to play the game.

Current FINAL FANTASY XIV players who wish to play on Nintendo Switch 2 If you already play FINAL FANTASY XIV on another platform, you will still need to purchase FINAL FANTASY XIV for Nintendo Switch 2 and a separate subscription for the Nintendo Switch 2 version of the game. However, if you have remaining game time on other platforms, the Nintendo Switch 2 subscription will be available at half price.

There will be an approximately one-month period of early access to ensure server stability, after which official service will start. A subscription to Nintendo Switch Online will not be required to play FINAL FANTASY XIV on Nintendo Switch 2 in either the early access period or during official service.

During the latest Nintendo Direct, Square Enix also announced the new HD-2D title, Final Fantasy Resonance for Switch and Switch 2. It's out this October.

Players will require a separate subscription from other platforms

items.title: Patch 7.51 Notes
items.summary: Patch 7.51 introduces the seventh installment of the ultimate raid series, Dancing Mad (Ultimate), as well as new custom deliveries featuring Tiisol Ja. And for our intrepid Disciples of the Hand and Land, new exploration awaits on Auxesia.
items.content:
Patch 7.51 introduces the seventh installment of the ultimate raid series, Dancing Mad (Ultimate), as well as new custom deliveries featuring Tiisol Ja. And for our intrepid Disciples of the Hand and Land, new exploration awaits on Auxesia. * Additional features will be implemented in future updates:
  • The Occult Crescent: North Horn, Weapon Enhancement Quests - Phantom Weapons, Inconceivably Further Hildibrand Adventures sidequests, and Allied Society capstone sidequests are scheduled to release in Patch 7.55.
  • Further main scenario quests and the beastmaster limited job are scheduled to release in Patch 7.56.
Read the full patch notes on the Lodestone. Launch FINAL FANTASY XIV Now

items.title: The Make It Rain Campaign Begins May 29!
items.summary: The Make It Rain Campaign returns once more to the Gold Saucer! With a 50% MGP bonus and an assortment of adorable otter-themed goods on offer, you won't want to miss out! Furthermore, campaign attendants will be offering a range of items a
items.content:
The Make It Rain Campaign returns once more to the Gold Saucer! With a 50% MGP bonus and an assortment of adorable otter-themed goods on offer, you won't want to miss out! Furthermore, campaign attendants will be offering a range of items at discounted prices, including some exclusive limited-time deals, so be sure to join the festivities and rake in the rewards while you can!

Event Duration

From Friday, May 29, 2026 at 1:00 a.m. (PDT) / 8:00 (GMT) / 9:00 (BST) / 18:00 (AEST) to Wednesday, June 24, 2026 at 7:59 a.m. (PDT) / 14:59 (GMT) / 15:59 (BST) / Thursday, 25 June at 0:59 (AEST)

How to Participate

Begin the quest "You Otter Be There" by speaking to Ollier in Ul’dah - Steps of Nald (X:9.4 Y:9.1). * Players must be level 15 and above to start the quest. * Players must first complete the quest "It Could Happen to You."

Rewards

* MGP rewards from the Gold Saucer have been increased by 50% for the duration of this event. * Certain items available during previous iterations of the event can be obtained via the campaign attendant. * The Crystal Tower striker, Monster Toss machine, Cuff-a-Cur machine, and a number of other prizes may be purchased at a reduced MGP cost from the campaign attendant. Visit the special site for more details.

items.title: Play Free for up to 4 Days in the Latest Free Login Campaign!
items.summary: In the hope that would-be adventurers can experience all that FFXIV has to offer, we are pleased to announce the latest Free Login Campaign. Follow the Trail to the Heavens ! In Patch 7.5, you can face Enuo in the Unmaking, take a stroll th
items.content:
In the hope that would-be adventurers can experience all that FFXIV has to offer, we are pleased to announce the latest Free Login Campaign. Follow the Trail to the Heavens! In Patch 7.5, you can face Enuo in the Unmaking, take a stroll through Windurst: The Third Walk in the FINAL FANTASY XI alliance raid crossover, and leap into battle in Archeia Harmonias, an all-new arena for Crystalline Conflict. A host of other additions and adjustments will make your time in Eorzea more streamlined and enjoyable, including increased furnishing placement limits, the consolidation of dyes, and additional pieces of gear that can now be converted to outfit glamour or stored in the armoire. With the introduction of Patch 7.5, the Shadowbringers expansion can now be played as part of the FINAL FANTASY XIV Starter Edition. The expansion will be automatically registered to your account if you already own the Starter Edition, so why not take this opportunity to jump into the adventure?

If you or any of your friends are currently taking a break from your adventures, you won't want to miss out on this limited-time event! Visit the Free Login Campaign website for details.

items.title: The Maiden's Rhapsody Returns!
items.summary: Experience the intriguing tale bridging Hydaelyn and Vana'diel as The Maiden's Rhapsody─a past collaboration with FFXI─returns to the realm once more! Embark on a journey with one of Vana'diel's heroes and discover what twists of fate broug
items.content:
Experience the intriguing tale bridging Hydaelyn and Vana'diel as The Maiden's Rhapsody─a past collaboration with FFXI─returns to the realm once more! Embark on a journey with one of Vana'diel's heroes and discover what twists of fate brought her to our realm. * This event is identical to the one previously held. * If you have already completed the event, you can replay the quests via the seasonal event replay feature. We highly recommend learning more about this feature on the collaboration special site.

Event Duration

From Friday, May 8, 2026 at 1:00 a.m. (PDT) / 8:00 (GMT) / 9:00 (BST) / 18:00 (AEST) to Monday, May 25, 2026 at 7:59 a.m. (PDT) / 14:59 (GMT) / 15:59 (BST) / Tuesday, 26 May at 00:59 (AEST)

How to Participate

Begin the quest "A Journey to Remember" by speaking to the Remumu in Limsa Lominsa - Upper Decks (X:11.6 Y:11.2).

Rewards

Visit the special site for more details.

items.title: Free trial now to include FINAL FANTASY XIV: Shadowbringers!
items.summary: Beginning Tuesday, April 28, with the release of Patch 7.5, the free trial will also include the award-winning FINAL FANTASY XIV: Shadowbringers. Spread the word and tell your friends, because there’s never been a better time to begin their
items.content: Beginning Tuesday, April 28, with the release of Patch 7.5, the free trial will also include the award-winning FINAL FANTASY XIV: Shadowbringers. Spread the word and tell your friends, because there’s never been a better time to begin their adventure! Check out the free trial. For those who have already registered FINAL FANTASY XIV: A Realm Reborn to their accounts, worry not─this change also applies to all existing owners as well. * Service accounts will be updated to reflect this change starting on Tuesday, April 28. Please note that the registration status update may not be displayed immediately on the Mog Station.
